lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Do you have any gold ore that you want me to take to the bank in the city?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have any, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have this one piece of ore...

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: That wouldn't do you much good, I'm afraid. My minimum fee is one piece of gold ore, there wouldn't be anything left for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, please take my gold to the city. I've got [number] pieces of ore right here.

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Are you sure you have enough space in your bank account for that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, you're right actually, I don't.

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Let's see, if I take that to the bank for you, I'd keep [number] for myself and bring [number] to the bank for you. Does that sound fair?

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Excellent, it's been good doing business with you! I'll take these to the city with my next shipment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, that's far too expensive for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'll hang on to these myself, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It all seems a bit expensive, really.

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: I don't know... it's just what Dondakan told me, if someone wanted me to ship their gold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's plenty of gold coming from that rock now, surely you can lower the price somewhat?

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Alright. Tell you what, I'll only keep [number] for myself and bring [number] ores to the bank for you. Deal?

lines.speaker: Dwarven Boatman
lines.text: Excellent, it's been good doing business with you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'll hang on to these myself, thanks.
